draft.summary: Review a repo's README against current code, summarize the diff, and update the README when drift is detected.
assumptions: Repository path is supplied at run start via the manual trigger input.
The README is named README.md at the repo root unless the user names a different file.
Updates are applied via a focused patch that preserves unrelated README content.
warnings: Auto-bumping the workflow version on every accepted refinement can produce a noisy history; publish deliberately.

fetch_readme.title: Read README and current code
fetch_readme.prompt: Read ${ input.repo_path }/${ input.readme_path } and the surrounding code. Return JSON only with keys: summary (string), drift (boolean), evidence (string).

apply.title: Apply README patch
apply.prompt: Edit ${ input.repo_path }/${ input.readme_path } so it matches the current code. Return JSON only with keys: summary (string), patch (string).

refine.summary: Added a reviewer step and a retry policy on the README fetch.
assumptions: A reviewer profile is available in the active Hermes profile list.
Retry is bounded so we surface persistent drift instead of looping.
warnings: Adding retry can mask persistent read errors; watch the run history the first few times this workflow executes.

fetch_readme.title: Read README and current code
fetch_readme.prompt: Read ${ input.repo_path }/${ input.readme_path } and the surrounding code. Return JSON only with keys: summary (string), drift (boolean), evidence (string).

review.title: Review README diff
review.prompt: Review the proposed README changes against the current code. Return JSON only with keys: approved (boolean), feedback (string).

apply.title: Apply README patch
apply.prompt: Edit ${ input.repo_path }/${ input.readme_path } so it matches the current code. Return JSON only with keys: summary (string), patch (string).
